headline: Bengaluru ends 2019 with robust office absorption; ORR retains dominance
body_text: Bengaluru closed calendar year 2019 on a strong note, recording approximately 12 million square feet of gross office space absorption across Grade-A buildings. The Outer Ring Road micro-market retained its position as the city's dominant office node, accounting for nearly 45 per cent of total leasing volume. IT and ITeS occupiers continued to anchor demand, with Global Capability Centres (GCCs) emerging as the fastest-growing occupier category. Embassy Office Parks REIT reported healthy occupancy levels across its Bengaluru properties. Brokerages expected the momentum to continue through early 2020, supported by strong hiring pipelines in the technology sector.

headline: COVID-19 Effect: Housing sales and new launches dip 42% each in Q1 2020
body_text: Residential sales across India's top seven cities saw a 42 per cent year-on-year drop in Q1 2020, according to ANAROCK Property Consultants. In Q1 2020, total sales stood at 45,200 units against 78,510 units a year ago. New launches fell an identical 42 per cent, from 70,480 in Q1 2019 to approximately 41,200 units. Bengaluru added approximately 8,600 new units in Q1 2020, a quarterly decrease of 18 per cent, with over 87 per cent of new supply in the sub-INR 80 lakh price bracket. The nationwide lockdown imposed on 25 March 2020 brought all construction and site activity to a halt, compounding the demand shock already evident in the quarter.

headline: Karnataka reduces stamp duty for affordable homes to revive registrations
body_text: The Government of Karnataka announced a reduction in stamp duty for sub-INR 20 lakh residential properties to 2 per cent, and for properties priced between INR 21 lakh and INR 35 lakh to 3 per cent. The relief measure, aimed at stimulating property registrations during the pandemic-induced slowdown, was welcomed by affordable housing developers. CREDAI Bengaluru stated that the measure would benefit buyers in the peripheral areas of the city where affordability thresholds were still largely within the INR 20-35 lakh range. However, analysts noted the impact would be limited in Bengaluru's primary market where median ticket sizes exceed INR 60 lakh.

headline: Home loan rates at decade low: How sub-7% borrowing is reshaping Bengaluru homebuyer sentiment
body_text: The Reserve Bank of India's accommodative monetary policy stance kept the repo rate at a record low 4 per cent through 2020, pushing home loan rates to below 7 per cent — a level not seen in over a decade. SBI's repo-linked home loan, launched at 6.95 per cent, effectively reduced EMIs for a INR 50 lakh, 20-year loan by approximately INR 3,200 per month compared to 2019 levels. In Bengaluru, real estate portals reported a 40 per cent month-on-month uptick in housing enquiries following the rate cut cycle. Analysts at PropEquity noted that improved affordability combined with growing preference for home ownership during the pandemic was creating a demand tailwind that would likely sustain through the festive season.

headline: Bengaluru leads Q2 2020 new launches with 49% share of top-city supply
body_text: Despite the nationwide lockdown, Bengaluru emerged as the top-performing city for new residential supply in Q2 2020, accounting for 49 per cent of total new launches across major Indian cities, according to CREDAI Bengaluru. The city's developers leveraged virtual tours, digital booking platforms, and flexible payment schemes to sustain momentum. Demand was concentrated in the affordable segment with 82 per cent of ANAROCK survey respondents in Bengaluru having booked homes either during the lockdown or shortly before it. CREDAI noted that the affordable segment in the city was seeing renewed traction from serious buyers, particularly those motivated by record-low home loan interest rates.

headline: Bengaluru real estate 2020 wrap: resilience through disruption
body_text: Indian residential real estate recorded a total of approximately 1.38 lakh unit sales across the top seven cities in 2020 — the weakest performance in recent years but a testament to the market's structural resilience, according to ANAROCK. Bengaluru maintained its position among the top three residential markets by sales volume despite the pandemic. Average residential prices across the top seven cities remained largely stable in 2020, with Bengaluru registering no material price movement on a yearly basis, unlike previous cycles where demand shocks translated into price corrections. Developer confidence, however, remained intact with launches recovering sharply in Q4 2020.

headline: Union Budget 2021: Section 80EEA extension and REIT reforms boost real estate sentiment
body_text: The Union Budget 2021-22 extended the additional INR 1.5 lakh tax deduction available under Section 80EEA for first-time homebuyers of affordable properties — a provision originally set to expire in March 2021 — until March 2022. The move provided much-needed certainty to buyers considering sub-INR 45 lakh properties across Bengaluru's peripheral corridors including Electronic City, Kanakapura Road and Yelahanka. Simultaneously, the removal of TDS on REIT distributions opened India's listed REIT market — anchored by Bengaluru's Embassy Office Parks REIT and Mindspace REIT — to a wider class of retail investors. Market participants called the twin measures a significant confidence booster for both residential affordable and commercial asset classes.
verified_datapoint: YES — Section 80EEA extended; REIT TDS removed Union Budget 2021-22

headline: Second COVID wave and Karnataka lockdown: residential launches fall 40 per cent in Q2 2021
body_text: A ferocious second wave of COVID-19 swept across Karnataka in April and May 2021, prompting the state government to impose fresh restrictions on movement and economic activity. New residential project launches in Bengaluru fell approximately 40 per cent quarter-on-quarter in Q2 2021. Construction activity was again suspended, leading to delivery delays on several ongoing projects. RERA Karnataka identified a total of 60 delayed projects in the city involving approximately 29,850 units worth over INR 22,276 crore, up sharply from 20 delayed projects in the previous year. Notwithstanding the Q2 shock, ANAROCK estimated Q2 2021 sales at approximately 3,560 units in Bengaluru, still 19 per cent higher than the corresponding Q2 2020 period.

headline: Why Bengaluru homebuyers are choosing 3BHK over 2BHK: The work-from-home effect
body_text: The pandemic-driven adoption of remote and hybrid working has fundamentally shifted homebuyer preferences in Bengaluru, according to multiple broker surveys conducted through mid-2021. Demand for homes with dedicated study rooms, home offices and utility areas pushed average unit size preferences upward by 12-15 per cent. NoBroker's demand analysis showed a 35 per cent surge in searches for 3BHK apartments above 1,500 sq ft compared to pre-pandemic patterns. Developers including Brigade Enterprises and Prestige Group reported 3BHK configurations accounting for over 55 per cent of bookings in their post-COVID project launches. Pricing for 3BHK units in Whitefield and Sarjapur Road corridors moved from the INR 90 lakh–1.2 crore range in 2019 to INR 1.1–1.5 crore in mid-2021.

headline: Namma Metro Purple Line reaches Whitefield: how BMRCL's east corridor is transforming property values
body_text: The commissioning of the first operational stations on the Namma Metro Purple Line extension toward Whitefield in late 2021 marked a pivotal moment for the east Bengaluru real estate market. Property portals reported a 10-15 per cent spike in residential enquiries along the Whitefield corridor within weeks of the announcement. Analysts at JLL noted that transit-oriented development premiums of 8-12 per cent were emerging within a 500-metre radius of metro station catchment areas. Developers accelerated project registration timelines to capitalise on the positive sentiment, with RERA Karnataka recording a 22 per cent increase in new registrations in east Bengaluru in Q4 2021 compared to Q3 2021.

headline: Bengaluru residential market 2021 in review: sales surge 50% to 43,181 units on pandemic rebound
body_text: Bengaluru's residential market staged a powerful rebound in 2021 with sales reaching 43,181 units for the calendar year, a jump of approximately 50 per cent over the pandemic-disrupted 2020 tally, according to PropEquity city-level data. The 3BHK configuration was the most in-demand unit type. Residential prices saw modest upward pressure with an estimated 2 per cent annual rise across the city, even as developers faced a 20 per cent increase in input costs driven by steel and cement price inflation. The second COVID wave's impact on Q2 2021 was completely offset by sharp recoveries in Q3 and Q4, with Q1 2021 sales alone at 8,670 units — the strongest Q1 since before the pandemic.

headline: RBI delivers emergency 40-bps rate hike: Bengaluru mortgage market braces for higher EMIs
body_text: The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee announced an off-cycle emergency rate hike of 40 basis points on 4 May 2022, launching what would eventually become a cumulative 250-bps tightening cycle. The move pushed home loan rates from the record-low range of 6.75-7 per cent seen through 2021 to approximately 8.4 per cent by year-end 2022, and further toward 9.5 per cent by early 2023. For a Bengaluru buyer taking a INR 75 lakh home loan over 20 years, the EMI increase over the cycle amounted to approximately INR 8,000 per month, raising the EMI-to-income ratio. The Knight Frank Affordability Index for Bengaluru, which tracks EMI-to-income ratios, had improved from 32 per cent in 2019 to 27 per cent by 2022 — analysts warned the rate hike cycle could partially reverse those gains.

headline: Rising construction costs drive Bengaluru apartment prices up 8-12%: CREDAI
body_text: Sustained input cost inflation through 2022 pushed residential property prices in Bengaluru upward by 8-12 per cent across most micro-markets. CREDAI Bengaluru attributed the rise primarily to steel prices touching INR 68,000 per metric tonne and cement averaging INR 400 per bag — increases of 35 per cent and 28 per cent respectively from pre-pandemic levels. Developers who had held prices stable through 2020 and 2021 began implementing phased price revisions from Q2 2022 onward. Despite the cost push, sales volumes remained resilient. Residential sales in Bengaluru surged 80 per cent year-on-year to 26,677 units in H1 2022, according to Knight Frank India, driven by the strong IT-sector employment boom and pent-up demand from the pandemic years.

headline: How GCCs are redefining Bengaluru's office demand landscape
body_text: Global Capability Centres accounted for over 35 per cent of Bengaluru's office space absorption in 2022, marking a structural shift in occupier composition. Colliers International noted that BFSI and Engineering & Manufacturing sectors had almost doubled their share of office leasing from 10-12 per cent in 2020 to around 16-20 per cent by 2023. US-headquartered technology and BFSI GCCs continued to dominate demand with 71 per cent of the GCC take-up, followed by EU-based centres. Bengaluru and Hyderabad together held a cumulative 60 per cent share of GCC leasing activity from 2020 to 2023, cementing Bengaluru's position as India's premier GCC destination. In H2 2023, gross leasing by GCCs reached a record 12.4 MSF nationally, the highest since 2020.

headline: Bengaluru residential market 2022: 60,391 units sold as prices rise 15%
body_text: Bengaluru's residential property market closed 2022 with 60,391 unit sales — approximately double the pandemic-trough 2020 volume — marking its strongest annual performance in several years, according to PropEquity. Prices rose by an estimated 15 per cent on a full-year basis according to ANAROCK data, fuelled by a combination of input cost inflation, strong IT-sector demand and depleting unsold inventory. The mid-segment (INR 40-80 lakh) remained the volume driver while premium configurations (INR 80 lakh-1.5 crore) saw the fastest sales velocity growth. India's top seven cities collectively recorded approximately 3.65 lakh unit sales in 2022, with MMR and Bengaluru driving the national recovery.

headline: Bengaluru office market 2022: Engineering & Manufacturing overtakes Tech in leasing share
body_text: In a notable sectoral shift, Engineering and Manufacturing (E&M) occupiers captured a 26 per cent share of Bengaluru's office leasing in 2023, surpassing the Technology sector's 22 per cent — a first for the city's traditionally tech-dominated market, according to Colliers India. BFSI occupiers also grew their footprint significantly. The trend reflected the diversification of Bengaluru's economic base beyond pure software services, with automotive R&D, aerospace, semiconductor design and manufacturing firms increasingly anchoring new office campuses. Overall, India's office market ended 2023 with 58.2 million sq ft of gross absorption across the top six cities, representing a 16 per cent year-on-year increase, with Bengaluru and Delhi NCR together contributing approximately half the national demand.

headline: Namma Metro Purple Line fully operational: Whitefield sees 20% property appreciation in 12 months
body_text: The full commissioning of the Namma Metro Purple Line's Whitefield extension in early 2023, enabling end-to-end metro connectivity from Challaghatta to ITPL Whitefield, transformed the eastern corridor's real estate dynamics. Property values in Whitefield appreciated by approximately 20 per cent in the twelve months following the line's completion according to multiple broker analyses. Residential absorption in Whitefield alone reached 12,900 units during the year, driven by IT professionals, NRI buyers and long-term investors. Grade-A office developments along the ITPL corridor saw a reduction in vacancy as metro connectivity improved talent pool accessibility for occupiers. Average residential prices in Whitefield moved into the INR 8,000-10,000 per sq ft range by mid-2023.

headline: Average residential price in Bengaluru up 15% YoY in 2023; premium segment leads
body_text: Bengaluru's residential market registered a 15 per cent year-on-year increase in average prices in 2023, the highest single-year gain since the pre-RERA era, according to ANAROCK. The premium segment (INR 80 lakh-1.5 crore) recorded the fastest sales velocity. New launches grew 18 per cent according to Knight Frank's annual data, the second-highest city-wise growth rate after Kolkata. India's top eight markets collectively achieved residential unit sales of 3.29 lakh units in 2023 — a ten-year high — with launches reaching 3.51 lakh units, also a decade peak, per Knight Frank. Total pan-India sales in ANAROCK's top-7 city universe reached 4,76,530 units in 2023, a 31 per cent annual jump.

headline: Flex workspace demand in Bengaluru crosses 3.7 MSF in 2023: WeWork India, Awfis lead expansion
body_text: The flexible workspace segment in Bengaluru absorbed 3.7 million sq ft of office space in 2023 — a 24 per cent increase from 2022 — driven by the hybrid work model's sustained adoption across the IT and BFSI sectors, according to Colliers India. WeWork India and Awfis were among the most active operators expanding managed office footprints. India's flexible workspace market stood at 61 MSF in 2023 and was projected to reach 126 MSF by 2028, according to Avendus, a private equity advisory firm. GCCs increasingly favoured managed flex formats for project-based teams and overflow capacity, making Bengaluru's ORR and SBD nodes the dominant flex absorption micro-markets. Colliers noted that large-format flex deals exceeding 1 lakh sq ft accounted for over 50 per cent of flex leasing in Bengaluru.

body_text: India's housing market achieved a record in 2023 with sales of approximately 4,76,530 units across the top seven cities, a 31 per cent year-on-year increase according to ANAROCK. MMR and Pune led the volume rankings, but Bengaluru remained in the top three by sales. New launches rose 25 per cent from approximately 3,57,640 units in 2022 to 4,45,770 units in 2023. Hyderabad recorded the highest price appreciation at 24 per cent, followed by Bengaluru at approximately 15 per cent. Analysts at ANAROCK Chairman Anuj Puri's team called 2023 'phenomenal' for the Indian housing sector, defying concerns about rising mortgage rates and global uncertainty. The luxury segment (above INR 1 crore) outgrew all other segments on a percentage basis.

headline: Bengaluru dominates India office 2024: 14.74 MSF leasing; 66% YoY surge
body_text: Bengaluru dominated India's office space market in 2024, recording 14.74 million square feet of leasing activity — a 66 per cent increase from 9.01 MSF in 2023 — according to JLL's annual office market report released on 3 January 2025. India's overall office space absorption reached 49.56 MSF in 2024, up 17.9 per cent from 42.01 MSF in 2023. Bengaluru posted its highest-ever fourth quarter absorption at 6.69 MSF, accounting for 36.1 per cent of India's total during October-December 2024. The year saw leasing activity across India's top seven cities reach 77.22 MSF in gross volume — the highest annual leasing ever recorded, up 22.6 per cent year-on-year. Samantak Das, Chief Economist at JLL India, noted that global companies drove 58.6 per cent of leasing activity.

headline: Cushman & Wakefield: Bengaluru led 89 MSF India office record in 2024; GCC demand historic
body_text: India's office sector closed 2024 with 89 million square feet of gross leasing volume across the top eight cities, the highest ever recorded, according to Cushman & Wakefield's year-end data. Bengaluru led with 25.93 MSF (29 per cent of India GLV) followed by Mumbai at 17.84 MSF and Delhi-NCR at 13.14 MSF. Net absorption — a measure of real demand expansion — hit a record 50 MSF, surpassing the pre-COVID 2019 peak by 7 MSF. Bengaluru's net absorption was 14.18 MSF, capturing 28 per cent of India's total. Despite record demand, supply of Grade-A office space struggled to keep pace with only approximately 45 MSF of new completions, causing vacancy to drop to around 16 per cent. Anshul Jain, CEO India at Cushman & Wakefield, described 2024 as a defining year for the sector.

headline: BDA revises setback rules: Bengaluru residential developers gain up to 15% more saleable area
body_text: The Bengaluru Development Authority's revised setback and floor area ratio norms, effective March 2024, permitted residential developers in select zones to increase saleable area by up to 15 per cent per plot. The rule change was aimed at improving housing supply in peripheral growth corridors while maintaining adequate open space. Developers active in North Bengaluru, Sarjapur Road and Yelahanka were among the primary beneficiaries, with several restructuring existing project plans to incorporate the increased permissible area. CREDAI Bengaluru projected the revision would add approximately 8,000-10,000 additional units to the pipeline over 18 months.

headline: Namma Metro Yellow Line fully operational from RV Road to Bommasandra; Electronic City realty surges
body_text: All 16 stations on Namma Metro's 19-kilometre Yellow Line connecting Rashtreeya Vidyalaya (RV) Road to Bommasandra via Electronic City became fully operational on 10-11 August 2024. Property portals immediately recorded a surge in residential search activity along the southern corridor. Average residential prices in Electronic City and Kanakapura Road appreciated by 8-12 per cent in the months following the line's commissioning. BMRCL reported strong passenger ridership in the initial weeks with Electronic City Phase 1 and Silk Board stations among the busiest interchange nodes. The Yellow Line's completion brought the total operational Namma Metro network to over 70 kilometres, reinforcing Bengaluru's position as a transit-connected city.

headline: Knight Frank Q1 2025: India residential sales strong; Bengaluru and NCR see price-driven moderation
body_text: India's real estate market remained strong in Q1 2025, with record office demand and rising residential sales, according to Knight Frank's India Real Estate Office and Residential Market report for January-March 2025. Office transactions reached 28.2 MSF nationally, led by Bengaluru. In the residential segment, home sales above INR 20-50 million surged 28 per cent year-on-year. Knight Frank experts noted that NCR and Bengaluru recorded the steepest price escalations, which weighed on overall homebuyer demand volumes even as premium-segment momentum continued. Separately, sales dipped slightly in Delhi-NCR, Bengaluru and Hyderabad in Q1 2025 compared to the same period last year, while Mumbai achieved a 5 per cent rise, Pune 20 per cent and Chennai 10 per cent.

headline: Bengaluru homes are getting bigger and pricier: Knight Frank H1 2025 confirms premiumisation
body_text: Bengaluru recorded the highest number of residential launches in recent history at 33,498 units in H1 2025 — a 31 per cent year-on-year increase compared to H1 2024 — according to Knight Frank's India Real Estate H1 2025 report. However, overall sales saw a modest 3 per cent year-on-year dip to 26,599 units, reflecting a realignment in buyer segment preferences rather than demand weakness. Homes priced between INR 1-2 crore accounted for 47 per cent of total Bengaluru sales, while the INR 2-5 crore bracket made up 24 per cent — together representing 79 per cent of all residential sales, a major structural shift from the affordable-segment dominance of 2018-2020. The average residential price in Bengaluru reached INR 8,720 per sq ft in Q2 2025 versus INR 4,700 per sq ft in Q2 2020, implying 79-85 per cent appreciation over five years.

headline: PropEquity: Bengaluru Q3 2025 residential sales up 21%; Jan-Sep tally reaches 49,559 units
body_text: Bengaluru's housing market is witnessing strong demand with sales estimated to rise 21 per cent in July-September 2025 to 16,840 units, compared with 13,966 units in Q3 2024, according to PropEquity. Real estate data analytics firm PropEquity's data showed total residential sales in Bengaluru's primary market are likely to increase to 49,559 units during the January-September 2025 period against 46,392 units in the same period of 2024, a year-on-year gain of approximately 7 per cent. Real estate developers expected the festive season in Q4 2025 to drive sales higher, anticipating a strong close to the year. India's top nine cities were however estimated to see a 4 per cent decline in Q3 2025 housing sales mainly due to lower demand in the Mumbai region and Pune.

headline: Five-year Bengaluru real estate retrospective: from ₹4,700 to ₹8,720 per sq ft; a 79-85% appreciation journey
body_text: Bengaluru's residential property market has delivered the strongest price appreciation among India's major metropolitan cities over the five years from 2020 to 2025, with values rising from approximately INR 4,700 per square foot in Q2 2020 to INR 8,720 per square foot in Q2 2025 — a 79 per cent increase, according to data compiled by Casagrand and NoBroker. The city's real estate price growth surpassed that of Delhi, Mumbai and Kolkata over the same period, underpinned by the sustained expansion of the IT and ITeS sector, unprecedented commercial office space absorption, metro infrastructure buildout and growing NRI investment. The Knight Frank Affordability Index for Bengaluru improved from 32 per cent in 2019 to 26 per cent in 2023, indicating households spent a declining share of income on home loan EMIs despite rising prices.

headline: Bengaluru Grade-A offices at 90.8% occupancy; ICRA forecasts FY2026 net absorption of 16-16.5 MSF
body_text: Bengaluru's Grade-A office market entered 2026 at elevated occupancy levels of approximately 90.8 per cent as of September 2025, supported by three consecutive years of record-breaking leasing activity, according to JLL and bangaloreproperties.com sourcing ICRA data. ICRA estimated Bengaluru's Grade-A office stock at approximately 263 MSF — roughly 27 per cent of the total across India's top six office markets. Net absorption was forecast at 16-16.5 MSF in FY2026, with occupancy expected to rise further to 89-89.5 per cent by March 2026. Average office rentals were projected to rise approximately 3-4 per cent annually in FY2025 and FY2026, with JLL projecting CBD rental growth of 6-7 per cent per year and strong increases in Whitefield and ORR micro-markets.
